title: Will Graham Platner drop out of the 2026 United States Senate election in Maine before Jul 14, 2026?

current.lean_note: ADVERSARIAL VETO: The forecast lacks sufficient evidence to support a high confidence in a 'yes' outcome. The absence of public information does not constitute evidence of low probability, and the divergence from the market suggests overconfidence. The posit
adversarial_concerns: The forecast assumes that the absence of public evidence equates to low probability, but this ignores the possibility of private or unreported factors that could influence Platner's decision.
The divergence from the market-implied probability (0.017) suggests overconfidence in the forecast's assessment.
There is no credible evidence or reasoning provided to justify a high confidence level in a 'yes' outcome when the market implies a much lower probability.
current.rationale_summary: There is currently no public evidence of any scandal or issue involving Graham Platner that would suggest he is likely to drop out of the 2026 Senate election in Maine before July 14, 2026. The lack of news coverage or reports from reputable sources indicates that no major developments have occurred that would compel him to withdraw. However, the absence of information does not rule out the possib
key_drivers: No public scandal or issue reported
No indication of internal pressures or health concerns
reference_classes: Candidates dropping out due to scandals
Candidates dropping out due to health or personal issues

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 3%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: Presumptive nominee, uncontested primary (Mills suspended), explicitly refusing to withdraw and claiming strength despite controversies. Frontrunner voluntary exit in a ~3.5wk window is ~1-3%; small tail for escalating scandal/party pressure.
reference_classes: frontrunner voluntary withdrawal in narrow window (very low)

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 6%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: Won nomination ~72% on June 9 with scandals already public, publicly defiant, party has no mechanism to force exit. Maine's ballot-replacement deadline is 5pm July 13 (inside the window), which mechanically concentrates any drop-out into these 3 weeks, lifting the tail from ~2% to ~6%; no fresh post-June-10 shock found.
reference_classes: just-nominated scandal candidate dropping in 3wk window <3%; deadline concentrates the mass
